$BTC continues ranging. Bitcoin is ranging since February 7th, and you can see how clean it's respected. As you can see, I highlighted the range high and low, and three deviations outside the range. What are range deviations? These are price moves where bigger players fill their orders. For example, the bullish deviations below the rangelow. They trap shorts and breakout traders by consolidating below the rangelow. They use this liquidity to fill their orders and then price goes back up. The same goes for the bearish deviation, people get trapped thinking it's a breakout. Bigger players absorb this liquidity to fill shorts, and boom, we drop back into the range. Shorts printed well the last couple of days, and I closed most of them for 60%+. My trading plan for next week is to add shorts if we get a higher retest, and I'll target the rangelow.
